items.title: 'Special player' Marmoush shows Man City options without Haaland
items.summary: Pep Guardiola needed to shake things up after his weary Manchester City side began to flag this season and Omar Marmoush served a reminder of his talent.

items.title: Bournemouth v Liverpool: Premier League – live
items.summary: Match at Vitality Stadium kicks off at 5.30pm (GMT) Live scores | Share your thoughts with Billy via email 2 min: Gomez goes long down the line to Salah, who scampers after it but hooks his right-footed cross into the fans behind the goal. Liverpool get things going in their off-white away kit. The weather is miserable. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: PSV komt nog niet tot grote kansen in openingsfase
items.summary: PSV staat zaterdagavond om 20.00 uur in het Philips Stadion tegenover NAC Breda. De ploeg van Peter Bosz won dertien Eredivisie-duels op rij en hoopt de veertiende aan die zegereeks toe te voegen. Volg de wedstrijd op de voet in deze VI Live.

items.title: Wintertransfers Eredivisie seizoen 2025/26: alle clubs op een rij
items.summary: De transferperiode is weer losgebarsten. In dit overzicht van VI vind je alle afgeronde, door clubs bevestigde, wintertransfers van de achttien Eredivisie-clubs. Clubs in Nederland kunnen tot en met 3 februari spelers aantrekken .

items.title: VI Live: Liverpool komt tien minuten voor tijd op 2-2, PEC spaart NEC
items.summary: Zaterdag staan er weer veel fraaie potjes in binnen- en buitenland op het programma. NEC krijgt bezoek van PEC Zwolle, FC Twente speelt tegen Excelsior en in het buitenland komen onder meer Liverpool, Manchester City, Bayern München en Real Madrid in actie. Volg het voetbal in de VI Live.

items.title: SC Heerenveen neemt afscheid van 'voorbeeldprof' Bochniewicz
items.summary: Pawel Bochniewicz keert terug naar zijn thuisland Polen. De centrale verdediger verlaat SC Heerenveen na ruim vijf jaar.

items.title: Guardiola blasts officials: 'We won despite them'
items.summary: Pep Guardiola launched into a stinging attack on referees following Manchester City's 2-0 win over Wolves.

items.title: NC Courage appoint Häcken boss Lind head coach
items.summary: The North Carolina Courage announced Mak Lind as the team's new head coach on Saturday.

items.title: Bayern stunned by Augsburg for 1st league loss
items.summary: Bayern Munich slumped to its first Bundesliga defeat of the season by 2-1 at home to Bavarian rival Augsburg on Saturday.

items.title: Infantino: Resale will drive up WC ticket prices
items.summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino said that many fans who have applied for tickets for the 2026 World Cup will be looking to sell them for profit on resale sites, thus driving up the cost of ticket prices.

items.title: Wrexham strike twice in injury time to stun QPR
items.summary: Wrexham scored twice in stoppage time to come from behind and pull off an incredible 3-2 victory at QPR.

items.title: Napoli trekt na vertrek Lang de portemonnee voor nieuwe aanvaller
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Steelers set to hire McCarthy as head coach
items.summary: The Pittsburgh Steelers are set to hire Mike McCarthy as their new head coach.

items.title: Motherwell thump Kilmarnock to record ninth home game without conceding
items.summary: Motherwell kept up their fantastic home form with a thumping 4-0 victory over Kilmarnock at Fir Park.

items.title: Nisbet and Keskinen doubles ease Aberdeen to victory
items.summary: Kevin Nisbet and Topi Keskinen both scored twice as Aberdeen ended a run of six Scottish Premiership games without a victory with a 6-2 win over Livingston.

items.title: From 'impossible task' to 'hope' - will West Ham stay up?
items.summary: Back-to-back Premier League wins have taken West Ham United to within two points of 17th.

items.title: Ref's 'courageous' call denies Man City penalty on Premier League debut
items.summary: Referee Farai Hallam was praised for choosing not to award Manchester City a penalty against Wolves on his Premier League debut.

items.title: Teen star Caluori scores four tries as Saracens overwhelm Newcastle
items.summary: Noah Caluori scored four tries as Saracens overwhelmed Newcastle 73-14 at StoneX Stadium.

items.title: Manchester City get back on track against Wolves despite VAR handball rarity
items.summary: After Pep Guardiola dropped Erling Haaland and Phil Foden, Manchester City enjoyed a first Premier League win in four games. It handed Wolves a first defeat in six and was notable for the referee, Farai Hallam, on top-flight debut, daring to stick with his decision not to award a penalty after being sent by VAR for a pitchside review. That was for a Yerson Mosquera handball and infuriated Pep Guardiola, though after City’s dire recent form a first three points since 27 December is what matters. Continue reading...

items.title: Wilson stunner snatches last-gasp victory for Fulham to punish Brighton
items.summary: Harry Wilson’s taste for the spectacular continues to decorate Fulham’s season. In added time, the Welshman’s free-kick whipped towards Bart Verbruggen, whose failure to keep the ball out completed a regrettable afternoon for goalkeepers. Fulham had snatched an unlikely win. When these teams fought out a draw on August’s opening day, Brighton rued missed chances and Fulham took theirs. The song remains the same. Fabian Hürzeler’s team reprised the mistakes that have kept them landlocked in mid-table. Continue reading...
